docs.title: shark-cli
docs.text: A professional, modular CLI for the Sharktech Cloud Provider API.

docs.title: What is shark-cli?
docs.text: shark-cli lets you manage your entire Sharktech Cloud infrastructure from the terminal — virtual machines, networks, load balancers, secrets, Kubernetes clusters, metrics, and more.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• 14 command groups covering all major OpenStack services
  • 100+ sub-commands for full lifecycle management
  • Rich terminal output — coloured tables powered by Rich
  • Shell auto-completion — Bash, Zsh, and Fish
  • Secure configuration — env vars take precedence; config file written with 0600 permissions

docs.title: Supported Services
docs.text: Service Command Backend Compute shark server Nova Flavors shark flavor Nova Images shark image Glance Networks shark network Neutron Key Pairs shark keypair Nova Volumes shark volume Cinder Security Groups shark security-group Neutron Floating IPs shark floating-ip Neutron Load Balancers shark loadbalancer Octavia Secrets shark secret Barbican Clusters shark cluster Magnum Metrics shark metric Gnocchi Service Catalog shark catalog Keystone

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install .
shark setup
shark server list

See the Getting Started guide for full installation and configuration instructions.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • A Sharktech Cloud account with API credentials

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pipPoetry 
pip install .

poetry install

After installation the shark command is available globally.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
You need credentials from your Sharktech Client Area (cloud service information page):

Field Description Example Auth URL Keystone endpoint https://cloud-xx.sharktech.net:5000 Username OpenStack user myuser Password OpenStack password — Domain ID OpenStack domain mydomain Project ID OpenStack project myproject

docs.title: Interactive setup
docs.text:
shark setup

This prompts for all fields and stores them in ~/.shark/config.yaml (permissions 600).

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
export SHARK_AUTH_URL="https://cloud-xx.sharktech.net:5000"
export SHARK_USERNAME="myuser"
export SHARK_PASSWORD="mypassword"
export SHARK_DOMAIN_ID="mydomain"
export SHARK_PROJECT_ID="myproject"

Tip

Environment variables always take precedence over the config file.

docs.title: Shell Auto-Completion
docs.text:
BashZshFish

Add to ~/.bashrc:

eval "$(_SHARK_COMPLETE=bash_source shark)"

Add to ~/.zshrc:

eval "$(_SHARK_COMPLETE=zsh_source shark)"

_SHARK_COMPLETE=fish_source shark > ~/.config/fish/completions/shark.fish

You can also run shark completion <shell> to display these instructions.

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
shark --version
shark catalog        # List available service endpoints
shark server list    # List your compute instances

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Browse the command reference for detailed usage of each service.

docs.title: Command Reference
docs.text:
shark-cli organises commands by OpenStack service. Every command supports --help for inline documentation.

shark --help              # List all command groups
shark server --help       # List all server sub-commands
shark server create --help  # Detailed help for a specific command

docs.title: Command Groups
docs.text: Group Commands Description server 28 Compute instances — full lifecycle flavor 1 List available flavors image 11 Glance image management network 21 Networks, subnets, ports & routers keypair 5 SSH key pair management volume 13 Block storage volumes & snapshots security-group 7 Security groups & rules floating-ip 6 Floating IP allocation & association loadbalancer 18 Load balancers, listeners, pools, members & health monitors secret 8 Secrets & containers (Barbican) cluster 10 Kubernetes clusters & templates (Magnum) metric 8 Metrics, measures & resources (Gnocchi) catalog 1 Service endpoint discovery

docs.title: Confirmation prompts
docs.text:
Destructive commands (delete, remove) ask for confirmation. Skip with -y:

shark server delete <id> -y
shark loadbalancer delete <id> --cascade -y

docs.title: Output format
docs.text:
All commands output rich, coloured tables. Pipe to less -R for paging:

shark server list | less -R

docs.title: Service Catalog — shark catalog
docs.text: List available service endpoints from the Keystone catalog.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description catalog List all service endpoints

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
shark catalog

This displays a table of all OpenStack services available in your project, with their type, name, and public endpoint URL. Useful for debugging connectivity or discovering available services.

docs.title: Clusters — shark cluster
docs.text: Manage Kubernetes clusters & cluster templates (Magnum).

docs.title: Clusters
docs.text: Command Description list List clusters show <id> Show cluster details create <name> Create a Kubernetes cluster delete <id> Delete a cluster resize <id> Resize worker node count kubeconfig <id> Show cluster API address and connection info

docs.title: Cluster Templates
docs.text: Command Description template-list List cluster templates template-show <id> Show template details template-create <name> Create a cluster template template-delete <id> Delete a cluster template

docs.title: Create a cluster template
docs.text:
shark cluster template-create k8s-template \
  --image <image-id> \
  --external-network <ext-net-id> \
  --coe kubernetes \
  --keypair my-keypair \
  --flavor <flavor-id> \
  --master-flavor <flavor-id> \
  --docker-volume-size 20 \
  --network-driver flannel \
  --label boot_volume_size=20 \
  --label boot_volume_type=ceph-ssd

docs.title: Create a cluster
docs.text:
shark cluster create my-k8s \
  --template <template-id> \
  --node-count 3 \
  --master-count 1 \
  --keypair my-keypair \
  --timeout 60

docs.title: Monitor cluster creation
docs.text: shark cluster show <cluster-id>

docs.title: Get kubeconfig
docs.text: shark cluster kubeconfig <cluster-id>

docs.title: Resize workers
docs.text:
shark cluster resize <cluster-id> --node-count 5

Labels

Use --label key=value (repeatable) on template-create to set Magnum driver labels. Common labels:

  • boot_volume_size — Root volume size in GB (required for boot-from-volume clouds)
  • boot_volume_type — Volume type (e.g. ceph-ssd)
  • kube_tag — Kubernetes version tag

docs.title: Flavors — shark flavor
docs.text: List available compute flavors (Nova).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description list List all available flavors

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
shark flavor list

Output columns: ID, Name, vCPUs, RAM (MB), Disk (GB).

docs.title: Floating IPs — shark floating-ip
docs.text: Manage floating IPs (Neutron).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description list List floating IPs show <id> Show floating IP details create Allocate a floating IP from an external network delete <id> Release a floating IP associate <id> Associate with a port disassociate <id> Disassociate from its port

docs.title: Allocate and associate
docs.text:
# Allocate from external network
shark floating-ip create --network <ext-network-id>

# Associate with a server port
shark floating-ip associate <fip-id> --port-id <port-id>

docs.title: Disassociate and release
docs.text:
shark floating-ip disassociate <fip-id>
shark floating-ip delete <fip-id> -y

docs.title: Images — shark image
docs.text: Manage Glance images.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description list List images show <id> Show image details create <name> Create an image (and optionally upload data) upload <id> Upload image data from a local file download <id> Download image data to a local file update <id> Update image properties (JSON-Patch) delete <id> Delete an image deactivate <id> Deactivate an image reactivate <id> Reactivate a deactivated image tag-add <id> <tag> Add a tag to an image tag-delete <id> <tag> Remove a tag from an image

docs.title: Upload an image
docs.text:
shark image create "Ubuntu 24.04" \
  --disk-format qcow2 \
  --container-format bare \
  --file ubuntu-24.04.qcow2

docs.title: Download an image
docs.text: shark image download <id> --output ./my-image.qcow2

docs.title: Update properties
docs.text: shark image update <id> --property os_distro=ubuntu

docs.title: Key Pairs — shark keypair
docs.text: Manage SSH key pairs (Nova).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description list List key pairs show <name> Show key pair details (fingerprint & public key) create <name> Generate a new key pair (returns private key) upload <name> Import an existing public key generate <name> Generate locally and upload the public key delete <name> Delete a key pair

docs.title: Generate and save a key pair
docs.text:
shark keypair create my-key > ~/.ssh/my-key.pem
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/my-key.pem

docs.title: Upload an existing public key
docs.text: shark keypair upload my-key --public-key ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

docs.title: Load Balancers — shark loadbalancer
docs.text: Manage load balancers, listeners, pools, members & health monitors (Octavia).

docs.title: Load Balancers
docs.text: Command Description list List load balancers show <id> Show load balancer details create <name> Create a load balancer delete <id> Delete a load balancer (supports --cascade)

docs.title: Listeners
docs.text: Command Description listener-list List listeners listener-show <id> Show listener details listener-create <name> Create a listener listener-delete <id> Delete a listener

docs.title: Pools
docs.text: Command Description pool-list List pools pool-show <id> Show pool details pool-create <name> Create a pool pool-delete <id> Delete a pool

docs.title: Members
docs.text: Command Description member-list <pool-id> List members in a pool member-add <pool-id> Add a member to a pool member-remove <pool-id> <member-id> Remove a member

docs.title: Health Monitors
docs.text: Command Description healthmonitor-list List health monitors healthmonitor-create <name> Create a health monitor healthmonitor-delete <id> Delete a health monitor

docs.title: Create a complete HTTP load balancer
docs.text:
# 1. Create the load balancer
shark loadbalancer create my-lb --subnet-id <subnet-id>

# 2. Wait for ACTIVE status
shark loadbalancer show <lb-id>

# 3. Create a listener
shark loadbalancer listener-create http-listener \
  --lb-id <lb-id> --protocol HTTP --port 80

# 4. Create a pool
shark loadbalancer pool-create web-pool \
  --listener-id <listener-id> \
  --protocol HTTP --algorithm ROUND_ROBIN

# 5. Add members
shark loadbalancer member-add <pool-id> \
  --address 10.0.0.10 --port 8080 \
  --subnet-id <subnet-id> --name web-1

shark loadbalancer member-add <pool-id> \
  --address 10.0.0.11 --port 8080 \
  --subnet-id <subnet-id> --name web-2

# 6. Add a health monitor
shark loadbalancer healthmonitor-create http-check \
  --pool-id <pool-id> --type HTTP \
  --delay 10 --timeout 5 --max-retries 3 \
  --url-path /health

docs.title: Cascade delete
docs.text:
Delete a load balancer and all child resources in one command:

shark loadbalancer delete <lb-id> --cascade -y

Note

Octavia requires the load balancer to be in ACTIVE provisioning status before most mutations. Wait for the status to settle between operations.

docs.title: Metrics — shark metric
docs.text: Query metrics, measures & resources (Gnocchi).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description resource-type-list List resource types resource-list List resources resource-show <id> Show resource details and its metrics list List metrics show <id> Show metric details (archive policy, granularity) measures <id> Get measures (datapoints) for a metric archive-policy-list List archive policies status Show Gnocchi processing status (admin only)

docs.title: Browse resources
docs.text:
# List all resource types
shark metric resource-type-list

# List instance resources
shark metric resource-list --type instance --limit 10

# Show a specific resource and its metrics
shark metric resource-show <resource-id> --type instance

docs.title: Query measures
docs.text:
# Get CPU metrics for the last 24 hours
shark metric measures <metric-id> \
  --start 2026-04-07T00:00:00 \
  --stop 2026-04-08T00:00:00 \
  --granularity 300

# Get memory usage
shark metric measures <metric-id> \
  --start -1h \
  --granularity 300

docs.title: Inspect a metric
docs.text:
shark metric show <metric-id>

This shows the metric's archive policy and available granularities. Always use a granularity that matches the policy.

Finding the right granularity

Use shark metric show <id> to see the archive policy. Each policy defines granularity/points pairs. For example, ceilometer-low-rate stores 8640 points at 5-minute (300s) granularity.

Note

The status command requires admin privileges and will return 403 Forbidden for regular users.

docs.title: Networks — shark network
docs.text: Manage networks, subnets, ports & routers (Neutron).

docs.title: Networks
docs.text: Command Description list List networks show <id> Show network details create <name> Create a network update <id> Update a network delete <id> Delete a network

docs.title: Subnets
docs.text: Command Description subnet-list List subnets subnet-show <id> Show subnet details subnet-create <name> Create a subnet subnet-delete <id> Delete a subnet

docs.title: Ports
docs.text: Command Description port-list List ports port-show <id> Show port details port-create Create a port port-update <id> Update a port port-delete <id> Delete a port

docs.title: Routers
docs.text: Command Description router-list List routers router-show <id> Show router details router-create <name> Create a router router-update <id> Update a router router-delete <id> Delete a router router-add-interface <id> Add a subnet interface router-remove-interface <id> Remove a subnet interface

docs.title: Create a network with subnet
docs.text:
shark network create my-network
shark network subnet-create my-subnet \
  --network-id <network-id> \
  --cidr 10.0.0.0/24 \
  --gateway 10.0.0.1

docs.title: Create a router and attach subnet
docs.text:
shark network router-create my-router \
  --external-gateway <ext-network-id>
shark network router-add-interface <router-id> \
  --subnet-id <subnet-id>

docs.title: Secrets — shark secret
docs.text: Manage secrets & containers (Barbican key-manager).

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text: Command Description list List secrets show <id> Show secret metadata create <name> Create a secret delete <id> Delete a secret get-payload <id> Retrieve secret payload

docs.title: Containers
docs.text: Command Description container-list List secret containers container-show <id> Show container details container-delete <id> Delete a container

docs.title: Store a password
docs.text:
shark secret create db-password \
  --payload "s3cret!" \
  --secret-type passphrase

docs.title: Store a symmetric key
docs.text:
shark secret create aes-key \
  --secret-type symmetric \
  --algorithm AES \
  --bit-length 256

docs.title: Retrieve a secret
docs.text: shark secret get-payload <secret-id>

docs.title: Store a certificate with expiration
docs.text:
shark secret create tls-cert \
  --payload "$(cat cert.pem)" \
  --payload-content-type "application/x-pem-file" \
  --secret-type certificate \
  --expiration 2026-12-31T23:59:59

Warning

Secret payloads are stored encrypted at rest in Barbican. The get-payload command retrieves the decrypted value — treat it as sensitive output.

docs.title: Security Groups — shark security-group
docs.text: Manage security groups & rules (Neutron).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description list List security groups show <id> Show details and rules create <name> Create a security group update <id> Update name or description delete <id> Delete a security group rule-add <sg-id> Add a rule rule-delete <rule-id> Delete a rule

docs.title: Create a security group with rules
docs.text:
shark security-group create web-sg --description "Web servers"

# Allow HTTP
shark security-group rule-add <sg-id> \
  --protocol tcp --port-min 80 --port-max 80 \
  --remote-ip 0.0.0.0/0

# Allow HTTPS
shark security-group rule-add <sg-id> \
  --protocol tcp --port-min 443 --port-max 443 \
  --remote-ip 0.0.0.0/0

# Allow SSH
shark security-group rule-add <sg-id> \
  --protocol tcp --port-min 22 --port-max 22 \
  --remote-ip 10.0.0.0/8

docs.title: Compute — shark server
docs.text: Manage compute instances (Nova).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description list List servers show <id> Show server details create <name> Create a server delete <id> Delete a server start <id> Start a stopped server stop <id> Stop (shut down) a server reboot <id> Reboot a server rebuild <id> Rebuild with a new image resize <id> Resize to a new flavor confirm-resize <id> Confirm a pending resize revert-resize <id> Revert a pending resize rename <id> <name> Rename a server lock <id> Lock a server unlock <id> Unlock a server pause <id> Pause a server unpause <id> Unpause a server suspend <id> Suspend a server resume <id> Resume a suspended server rescue <id> Enter rescue mode unrescue <id> Exit rescue mode shelve <id> Shelve a server unshelve <id> Unshelve a server password <id> Retrieve admin password console <id> Open a VNC console URL list-volumes <id> List attached volumes list-interfaces <id> List network interfaces attach-interface <id> Attach a network interface detach-interface <id> Detach a network interface

docs.title: Create a server
docs.text:
shark server create my-vm \
  --flavor <flavor-id> \
  --image <image-id> \
  --network <network-id> \
  --keypair my-keypair \
  --security-group default

docs.title: Lifecycle operations
docs.text:
shark server stop <id>
shark server start <id>
shark server reboot <id> --hard

docs.title: Resize workflow
docs.text:
shark server resize <id> --flavor <new-flavor-id>
# Wait for VERIFY_RESIZE status
shark server confirm-resize <id>
# Or revert:
shark server revert-resize <id>

docs.title: Network interfaces
docs.text:
shark server list-interfaces <id>
shark server attach-interface <id> --net-id <network-id>
shark server detach-interface <id> --port-id <port-id>

docs.title: Volumes — shark volume
docs.text: Manage block storage volumes & snapshots (Cinder).

docs.title: Volumes
docs.text: Command Description list List volumes show <id> Show volume details create <name> Create a volume update <id> Update name or description delete <id> Delete a volume extend <id> Extend to a larger size retype <id> Change volume type set-bootable <id> Set or unset bootable flag set-readonly <id> Set or unset read-only flag

docs.title: Snapshots
docs.text: Command Description snapshot-list List volume snapshots snapshot-show <id> Show snapshot details snapshot-create Create a snapshot snapshot-delete <id> Delete a snapshot

docs.title: Create and attach a volume
docs.text:
shark volume create data-vol --size 50 --type ceph-ssd
# Attach via server command:
shark server attach-volume <server-id> --volume-id <volume-id>

docs.title: Create a snapshot
docs.text: shark volume snapshot-create --volume-id <id> --name my-snapshot

docs.title: Extend a volume
docs.text: shark volume extend <id> --new-size 100
